articles.title: WANT TO REACH TLDR'S AUDIENCE?
articles.summary: Fortune 50 companies like AWS and Google Cloud and fast growing startups like Vercel and Secureframe work with TLDR to reach our audience of over 4 million tech-savvy decision makers with highly customized campaigns (like this one!).

articles.title: HOW AI APPS MAKE MONEY
articles.summary: We're at the early stages of AI app development, with many products still striving to find product-market fit. While innovating pricing models is challenging and often not the initial focus, most AI products adopt a strategy that focuses on making pricing predictable and ensuring it does not deter potential users.

articles.title: ADJACENCY MATRIX: HOW TO EXPAND AFTER PMF
articles.summary: Expansion after achieving PMF involves evaluating whether existing channels are maximized, market saturation is approached, or diversification is needed. Assessing adjacency, with a focus on impact over cost, helps pinpoint viable directions, ensuring alignment with strengths and minimizing risk.

articles.title: WRITER-BUILDER
articles.summary: The emergence of "writer-builders" reflects a trend where individuals excel in both writing and building within their domains, enhancing innovation and cross-disciplinary understanding. Bridging creative thinking with practical execution advocates for career fluidity and the integration of reflective writing into professional practice.

articles.title: THE EVOLUTION OF THE PM-DESIGNER-TECH RELATIONSHIP
articles.summary: The roles of engineers, designers, and product managers are increasingly overlapping, driven by a deeper involvement in each other's domains and accelerated by AI. Cross-disciplinary skills and collaborative approaches are required. Blending roles without overstepping is key to leadership in product development.

articles.title: WHEN NOTIFICATIONS REMIND US OF THINGS WE'D RATHER FORGET
articles.summary: Notifications constantly vie for our attention, often intruding into our daily lives. Although users can adjust their settings to control the influx of notifications, app developers typically have little motivation to make these options more user-friendly. Their primary goal is to maximize user engagement with their products, which can conflict with the desire for less disruptive notifications.

articles.title: HOW I INTERVIEW PRODUCT DESIGNERS
articles.summary: A refined hiring process for product designers focuses on meticulous portfolio reviews, managerial screenings for soft skills, and comprehensive case studies to ensure candidates match the team's high standards. The process involves rigorous evaluations and team consensus to maintain design excellence and team synergy.

articles.title: BASIC GUIDE ON UX LAWS THAT EVERY PRODUCT MANAGER NEEDS TO KNOW
articles.summary: Jacob's Law, Hick's Law, the goal gradient effect, and the Von Restorff effect are key UX principles that guide PMs in creating products that are not only functional and visually appealing, but also intuitive to use.

articles.title: WHAT WE GET WRONG ABOUT INFLUENCE
articles.summary: True influence in leadership involves supporting others' goals and building mutual growth. Effective PMs gain influence not through authority but by being knowledgeable, accessible, and empathetic, fostering trust and collaboration within teams.

articles.title: SHINY TOY SYNDROME
articles.summary: Shiny Toy Syndrome, characterized by the fleeting satisfaction of new tools or technologies, often detracts from productivity and strategic focus. Common symptoms include frequent switching of tools and the lure of buzzworthy features. It can be addressed by implementing strategies such as cooling-off periods, rigorous decision-making processes, and reflection on the actual business needs and impacts of new implementations.

articles.title: 6 FRICTION POINTS BETWEEN PRODUCT MANAGERS & PRODUCT MARKETERS
articles.summary: Shifting launch dates, unclear user personas, and misaligned go-to-market strategies are common issues that can arise between PMs and PMMs when they're not aligned.

articles.title: WHAT A PRODUCT VISION IS NOT
articles.summary: A product vision is distinct from a company vision and entails a detailed description of the intended solution a company plans to build to achieve its broader goals.

articles.title: WHAT TO DO WHEN STRATEGY & PRIORITIES KEEP CHANGING EVERY WEEK (2 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Frequent changes in strategy may indicate a lack of conviction.
